W O T * ^ ^ RTcT^FT^t srf^R" ^SRTSW (^l^ld.f^rfcT Wcfa" ^FHRI, R+HH ^RT£W 5,000 ^o 2,500 ^o 1,000^0 200 ^o 5,000 ^ 2,500^0 1,000 *o 200 ^o 3. w w f ^ r *fr£ s^HiTHfi 41 Rd (Wf t ) 5ft tft 3TC t ^t, ^ R u i ^ k f^TcT ^ t i t 18 3T.^.(3TR.TT.^t.) 1,50,000 -- 2 of 4 -- 14 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] 1,000*0* @ ^PT srf^f^TT, 1954 % f^HT 36 % 3T^TR I * ^ p r ^ r i : ^ f % f ^ ) 1955 ^ t ^ l t f t r 52 ^ S T ^ T R I" I pBT. t . ^t-12012/1/2016-^TFT (^)] f ^ w : *j*r f^PT ^TR^T % <MM^, s^rraT^r, t , ^rrccr tK+k, STTSI^HI ^.^rr. ^T. f%. 364(ST), CTRT^ 14 srnTcr, 1996 ^t ^Rf^Ri 1%XT TTIT §• affc tr^i^dT w t o srf^g^rr #. m.^rr.R 606(3T), WTCT^ 13*prrt, 2000 ^RT f^TT ^FIT I NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 25th October, 2016 G.S.R. 1012(E).—Whereas certain draft rules further to amend the Tea Board (Write off Losses) Rules, 1996 were published, as required by sub-section (1) of section 49 of the Tea Act, 1953 (29 of 1953), in the Gazette of India vide notification of the Ministry of Commerce and Industry (Department of Commerce) number G.S.R.468(E) dated the 3rd August, 2016, inviting objections or suggestions from all persons likely to be affected thereby, within a period of thirty days from the date on which the Gazette in which the said notification was published were made available to the public; And Whereas the copies of the said Gazette was made available to the public on the 5th August, 2016; And Whereas no objections or suggestions were received from the public by the Central Government; Now, Therefore, in exercise of the powers conferred by clause (ja) of sub-section (2) of section 49 of the Tea Act, 1953, the Central Government hereby makes the following rules, namely:- 1. (1) These Rules may be called the Tea Board (Write off Losses) Amendment Rules, 2016. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. In the Tea Board (Write off Losses) Rules, 1996 (hereinafter referred to as the principal rules),- (i) in rule 2,- (a) in clause (e), for the words "Chief Regional Executive", the words "Executive Director" shall be substituted; (b) in clause (g), the word "Chairman", shall be omitted. In the principal rules, in rule 5, in sub-rule (2), for the words "Chief Regional Executive", the words "Executive Director" shall be substituted. 4. In the principal rules, in rule 4 and rule 6, for the word "Chairman", wherever it occurs, the words "Deputy Chairman" shall be substituted. 5. In the principal rules, in rule 8, the word "Chairman" wherever it occurs, shall be omitted. 6. In the principal rules, for the "Schedule", the following Schedule shall be substituted, namely:- 4. 3Fir gr^fa 3WTCT £ l M -- 3 of 4 -- [•MHI II-Vsl^ 3(i)] SI. Nature of Loss No. Authority "HKd *l <MH=I : 3HIMKU| "SCHEDULE Monetary Limit upto which the loss may be written off in each case 15 2. 3. Irrecoverable Losses of Stores or money including Stamps and Library Books Irrecoverable loans or advances Condemnation of Motor Vehicles Board Committees (Executive, Export Promotion Committee, Development Committee, Labour Welfare Committee) Dy. Chairman Secretary Board Committees (Executive, Export Promotion Committee, Development Committee, Labour Welfare Committee) Dy. Chairman Secretary Rs.5,000/- Rs.2,500/- Rs. 1,000/- Rs.200/- Rs.5,000/- Rs.2,500/- Board Rs. 1,000/- Rs.200/- (in years) whichever is reached later, have been fixed as under: Type of Vehicle Kms. Years Motor Vehicles fitted with less than 18 hp (RAC) 1,50,000 4. Other Monetary Dy. Losses Chairman Rs. 1,000/-* In case of irrecoverable rent damages (including furniture hire charges and service charges) in respect of Board's residential accommodations. @ As per Tea Rule 36 of Tea Rules, 1954 * As per Tea Board Bye Laws 52 of Tea Board Bye Laws, 1955.". [F. No. T-12012/1/2016-Plant (A)] ALOK VARDHAN CHATURVEDI, Addl. Secy. Note : The principal rules were published in the Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, Section 3, Sub-section (i), vide number G.S.R. 364 (E), dated the 14th August, 1996 and subsequently amended vide number G.S.R. 606(E), dated 13th July, 2000.
